notes: Source is the official Q-SYS Help documentation (help.qsys.com), corroborated by the QSC A&E specifications text file. The Core 110f has two hardware revisions: the original and v2. The v2 removes the front-panel OLED display, the 16-channel GPIO input/output euroblock connectors, the 12VDC output euroblock, and reduces front USB-A ports — leaving only the audio, RS232, HDMI, RJ11, dual RJ45, USB-B device, and rear USB-A host ports. The GPIO ports listed here are present on the original Core 110f only. The Tel Line port uses an RJ11 (6P2C) connector — mapped to 'rj45' as the closest available enum. HDMI is a standard output connector used solely for boot/diagnostic display; version not specified in any official documentation. USB speed grades not specified in official docs (USB 2.0 assumed for audio bridging use case but not confirmed). Euroblock audio connectors are 3.5mm pitch terminal blocks, not 3.5mm TRS jacks.

ports.name: Mic/Line In 1–8
ports.note: 8 channels; 3-position, 3.5mm pitch euroblock (orange); balanced/unbalanced; phantom power supported

ports.name: Flex 1–8
ports.note: 8 channels; 3-position, 3.5mm pitch euroblock (blue); software-configurable as input or output in any combination; phantom power on inputs

ports.name: GPIO In 1–16
ports.note: 16 channels; 10-position, 3.5mm pitch euroblock (black); 0–24V analog input or contact closure; present on original Core 110f only — removed in v2 revision

ports.name: GPIO Out 1–16
ports.note: 16 channels; 10-position, 3.5mm pitch euroblock (black); open collector 24V, 0.2A max; present on original Core 110f only — removed in v2 revision

ports.name: 12VDC Out
ports.note: 10-position euroblock (black); 12VDC, 0.2A max per channel; present on original Core 110f only — removed in v2 revision

ports.name: RS232
ports.note: 3-pin, 5mm pitch euroblock; RS-232 transmit and receive for legacy control

ports.name: HDMI Video Out
ports.note: Standard HDMI; used for diagnostics/boot-process display only — not a general video output; HDMI version not specified in documentation

ports.name: Tel Line
ports.note: RJ11 6P2C connector for POTS (Plain Old Telephone Service) telephony; closest enum is rj45 — actual connector is RJ11/6P2C

ports.name: LAN A
ports.note: Primary Q-LAN network connection; 1000 Mbps only

ports.name: LAN B
ports.note: Secondary/redundancy Q-LAN network connection; 1000 Mbps only

ports.name: USB-A Host (Front) 1–2
ports.note: 2× USB Type-A host ports on front panel

ports.name: USB-A Host (Rear) 1–4
ports.note: 4× USB Type-A host ports on rear panel; marked 'for future use' in early documentation

ports.name: USB-B Device
ports.note: 1× USB Type-B device port; provides AV-to-USB bridging (up to 16×16 audio + video); reduces networked audio channel count when active

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: IEC C14 inlet; 100–240V ~50/60 Hz, 150W max; internal universal power supply
